---
name: Paper enhancement
about: Propose a paper-level enhancement (new sections, appendices, revisions)
labels: enhancement, paper
---

## Paper enhancement

**Affected paper repository** (check one or more)

- [ ] `dcl` -- Paper I (*Geometry First*)
- [ ] `dcl-sm-derivation` -- Paper II (*Geometry Forces Physics*)
- [ ] `dcl-generator-zoo` (catalogue paper)
- [ ] `dcl-paper-03-tidal-ionization` -- Paper III
- [ ] new paper (planned / proposed): _______________________

**Affected or proposed section(s) / appendix**

Existing or new sections / appendices.  Use the file paths
(`paper/sections/<topic>.tex`).

**Type of enhancement** (check one or more)

- [ ] revise existing section
- [ ] add new section
- [ ] add new appendix
- [ ] reorganise structure
- [ ] add citations / bibliography entries
- [ ] clarify exposition (no new content)
- [ ] add / regenerate figure
- [ ] update audit table (claim or evidence)
- [ ] other:

**Current gap or problem**

What is missing, weak, unclear, or incomplete in the current text.

**Proposed enhancement**

Description of the proposed addition or change.

**Motivation**

Why this enhancement matters.

**Audit-table impact** (if applicable)

- New audit row?  `[ ] yes  /  [ ] no`.  If yes, draft claim line:
- Existing audit row(s) affected:
- Status change: `<old>` -> `<new>` (`PASS` / `PART` / `STUB` / `FAIL`)
- [ ] **Regression flag**: this changes a previously-`PASS` row
  to `FAIL` or `PART` (treat as critical)

**References / related material**

Papers, notes, issues, or links that inform this change.  Cite
upstream notes (`notes/<topic>.md` or
`external/research/Notes/...`) and follow-on catalogue entries.

**Dependencies / related issues**

List dependencies and related issues.

**Expected outcome**

What a successful result looks like.  How will reviewers know
the gap is closed?

**Additional context**

Any other context.
